title: cyperus rigens subsp. serrae
intro: Cyperus rigens subsp. serrae, commonly known as the 'Yellow Papyrus Sedge', is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and treating wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments.
